mnemonics: DICE: Decrease Input, Conquer Easily. Think of reducing a problem by one element (like rolling a die to remove one number) before solving the simpler case.
keyPoints: The approach solves a problem by reducing its size by a constant factor (often by 1) and then extending the solution of the smaller instance to the larger one.
It has three main variants: Decrease-by-a-constant (e.g., Insertion Sort), Decrease-by-a-constant-factor (e.g., Binary Search), and Variable-size-decrease (e.g., Euclid's algorithm).
It is often implemented recursively, but can also be done iteratively.
commonMistakes: Confusing it with Divide-and-Conquer. Decrease-and-Conquer reduces the problem by one element; Divide-and-Conquer splits it into multiple subproblems.
Incorrectly implementing the base case for the decrease, leading to infinite recursion or incorrect results for the smallest instance.
